english: (cog. to ltáb-pa (ltab pa)), to double down, to turn in, mtha (mtha), or sné-mo (sne mo) to hem, by turning in the edge, cf. sné-mo (sne mo).
humour, whim, caprice, ltem-rgyáṅ byéd-pa (ltem rgyang byed pa) to be whimsical or capricious Cs.
the state of being full, e.g. a vessel full of water; full, overflowing, ltém-po (ltem po) full; ltem-ltém (ltem ltem) so full that it runs over.

seldom ltó-ba (lto ba) (C., Mil.) 1. food, victuals, lto(b)za-ba (lto (b)za ba) 1. to eat, lto yaṅ ma zos (lto yang ma zos) he did not eat anything Glr.; 2. to gain or get one's living C.; ltó-la byin (lto la byin) give him to eat! Lt.; lúg-la lto ster (lug la lto ster) feed the sheep; lto rgyó-ba (lto rgyo ba) to prepare food Mil.; *to nyo srog tsoṅ* (*to nyo srog tsong*) C. he risks his life in order to procure food; gla-ltó (gla lto) wages and food; lto-gós (lto gos), lto-rgyáb (lto rgyab), food and clothes Mil.; lto-rgyab-skyíd (lto rgyab skyid) Lex. prob. food, clothes, and good health (comfort); *dha tó-če za gyu yin* (*dha to che za gyu yin*) C. now I will go and eat (something). -- lto-čuṅ (lto chung), lto-ráṅ (lto rang) Sch.: a person temperate in eating. -- ltó-˳dun-can (lto 'dun can) an epicure, parasite, sponger. -- lto-źiṅ (lto zhing) provision ground which a person receives for his subsistence. -- źim-lto-can (zhim lto can) dainty-mouthed, lickerish. -- 2. goat's beard, Tragopogon, used as a kitchen-vegetable.

belly, stomach; also the belly of a bottle; ltó-ba sá-la ˳bébs-pa (lto ba sa la 'bebs pa) to prostrate one's self. Comp. lto-gáṅ (lto gang) a full belly, also: with a full belly or stomach. -- ltó-˳gro (lto 'gro), ltós-˳gró (ltos 'gro) 1. moving or creeping on the belly, a worm, a snake. 2. symb. num.: 8. -- lto(-ba)-˳gróg(-pa) (lto(-ba)-'grog(-pa)) Cs.: 'belly-fretting, a nervous excitement of the belly'. -- lto-stóṅ (lto stong) with an empty stomach, jejune, empty. -- lto-ldír (lto ldir) belly of a vessel, ltó-ldir-can (lto ldir can) swelling out, bellied, like vessels. -- ltó-na-ba (lto na ba), ltó-zug (lto zug) stomach-ache. -- lto-˳ṕyé (lto 'phye) crawling or creeping on the belly, a snake; lto-˳ṕye cén-po (lto 'phye chen po), a fabulous monster of the serpent kind, similar to the klu.
a demon Sch.; dre-ltógs (dre ltogs) prob. the same

I. vb. 1. to be hungry, ltógs-so (ltogs so) I am hungry Cs., ltógs-su ˳bór-ba (ltogs su 'bor ba) to suffer a person to hunger, to starve Dzl. -- 2. Sch.: to regret, ltogs nyal ma byeb do (ltogs nyal ma byeb do) not always lie in grief and regret! Sch.(?); ltógs-par bźugs-pa (ltogs par bzhugs pa) resp. to be full of regret. II. sbst. hunger. III. adj. hungry, séms-can ltógs-pa-rnams (sems can ltogs pa rnams) Dzl.; ltógs-par gyúr-ba (ltogs par gyur ba) to grow hungry; ltógs-gri (ltogs gri) Mil., col. *ltóg-ri* (*ltog ri*) W. hunger. *ṅa(-la) ltóg-ri rag* (*nga(-la) ltog ri rag*) I am hungry, *kyod(-la) ltóg-ri rag* (*khyod(-la) ltog ri rag*) you are hungry, *ko ltóg-pa yod* (*kho ltog pa yod*) he is hungry. -- ltogs-ṕyúg (ltogs phyug) hunger (i.e. poverty) and wealth Glr. -- ltog-tsór (ltog tshor) the feeling of hunger, ltogs-tsor če (ltogs tshor che) I am very hungry Mil.
notch, incision, indentation, mdá-ltoṅ (mda' ltong) the notch in an arrow; a depression, rí-tóṅ (ri tong) in a ridge of mountains, la-tóṅ (la tong) the indentation of a mountain-pass.
sras-ltór (sras ltor) a bastard prince Glr.

1. vb., = ltá-ba (lta ba), to look at, on, or to, ccd., gnyén-po-la ma ltós-par (gnyen po la ma ltos par) without looking to a spiritual guide Thgy.; kyod dé-la ltos mi dgos-pa źig yin (khyod de la ltos mi dgos pa zhig yin) you need not care for that Mil.; ré-źiṅ ltós-pa (re zhing ltos pa) Glr. to look at (a thing) hopefully; dé-la ltós-na (de la ltos na) if I look at, consider, this Mil., if one compares this with... Thgy.; *(s)ṅá-ltos či-čug* (*(s)nga ltos chi chug*) (?) *tsáṅ-ma čó-k̇an* (*tsang ma cho mkhan*) W. a person acting with great circumspection -- 2. sbst. the looking at or on, ltós-pa méd-par (ltos pa med par) without looking at it (e.g. in playing at dice); relation, respect, regard Cs.
preparation, arrangement, sta-gón byéd-pa (sta gon byed pa) to make preparations, to prepare, arrange, fit out; ˳tsó- ('tso-)
